steps.title: Identify what wins over Richard's heart
steps.content: The passage states: “This affectionate willingness to fall in with his own plans quite won the old man's heart.” This sentence is the passage's turning point—it explains the direct cause of Richard's warmth toward Adam.

steps.title: Match this cause to the answer choices
steps.content:
Choice A correctly identifies the passage's main purpose: how Adam's qualities lead to closeness.

The others miss this core dynamic, instead focusing on peripheral details—logistics of sleeping on the ship (B), excitement for the journey (C), or fabricating initial wariness (D) that contradicts the “auspiciously begun” opening.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
